 96 Source: jira | Key: LIGHT-34 | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-16T21:16:25.504+0800 | Status: Backlog (To Do) | Type: Story | Priority: High | Reporter: Nikhil Grover | Labels: ad-tech-tracking, osmos-integration, retail-media, architecture-governance, acceptance-criteria | cloners: LIGHT-70 | parent: LIGHT-394
 97 Michael Bui actively engaged with Jira ticket LIGHT-34 ('Tracking for all display content'), a High Priority Story under parent LIGHT-394 (RMN integration). As the engineering lead governing ad tech standards and backend architecture, Michael raised critical clarifications on OSMOS event verification logic and user consent requirements. He specifically questioned implementation details regarding timing resets when users switch browser tabs versus using mobile back-navigation buttons. His input evaluated the technical feasibility of tracking impressions based on viewport duration (1s for images, 2s for video) and click events across various display modules like banners and search bars. Michael's analysis suggests he is determining if the current acceptance criteria sufficiently handle edge cases before approving the move from Backlog to active development.

101 Jira ticket RM-697 (Bug: High priority) assigned to Michael Bui concerns a missing ad label for the first product in the 'Testhas Offer swimlane' on the Web OG Home page. Reported by Madhuri Nalamothu on Jan 5, the issue was isolated to the web platform, as mobile versions functioned correctly. Michael actively engaged with the ticket, first requesting verification on Jan 6 noting he saw the ad displaying, and later confirming resolution via QE team testing on Jan 14. The ticket status is 'Done' following validation by Milind Badame on Apr 17. This represents a direct ownership scenario where Michael was assigned to resolve a production-relevant bug affecting e-commerce advertising visibility.
102 
103 ## [26/26] Transition to Impression-Based Inventory & Multi-Banner Delivery
104 Source: jira | Key: DPD-838 | Relevance: 8/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T14:57:54.635+0800 | Status: TESTING IN PREPRODUCTION (In Progress) | Type: Story | Priority: High | Assignee: Chee Hoe Leong | Reporter: Nikhil Grover | Labels: retail-media, ad-tech, architecture-governance, slo-validation, banner-delivery | parent: DPD-385
105 Jira ticket DPD-838 tracks the high-priority transition of Retail Media Network (RMN) ad inventory from fixed tenancy to an impression-based model supporting up to 20 banners per request. Currently in 'Testing in Preproduction', the story involves orchestration across OSMOS, DBP, and MOE services with specific logic for 'Endemic Boosting' and slot deduplication.
106 
107 A critical architectural pivot occurred on April 15, shifting endemic boosting logic prior to slot deduplication within the 'au' service. This change directly impacts production reliability for key pages Michael governs: Omnipresence, Search, and Checkout. Although not explicitly tagged, Michael's role as Engineering Governance lead necessitates validation that this modification preserves SLO targets and prevents regressions.
108 
109 Action Required: Formal confirmation from Michael on whether the modified logic poses risks to existing SLOs for key RMN pages before proceeding with production deployment.
